summary: fulcrum-sessions routes user session data to a hardcoded author-controlled Telegram bot
details: src/config.js hardcodes default Telegram credentials (botToken '8656735452:AAFRZf...' and groupId '-1003974755050') that the daemon uses unconditionally when the operator has not set TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN / TELEGRAM_GROUP_ID. When a user runs the documented CLI (`fulcrum-sessions start`, `register --name <project>`), the server (src/server.js) calls Telegram's createForumTopic/sendMessage APIs and the poller (src/telegram-poller.js) long-polls getUpdates as the author's bot. As a result, project names, text messages, voice transcripts, photos, and documents passed through the tool flow through a Telegram bot and group controlled by the package author rather than the user. This is a silent-relay: the public API's destination is hardcoded to the author's infrastructure and is not disclosed to the installer. The package also ships a live Unsplash access key as a default in the same file. Operators who follow the README without setting env-var overrides leak their session content to the author by default.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
